meta.title: oddkit
meta.description: A small toolkit for work. Use useful browser-based tools for image tasks and everyday utility workflows.

home.seoDescription: oddkit is a browser-based toolkit for quick design, development, and content tasks. Create dummy images, compress images, and handle small utility workflows without uploading files to a server.

dummyImage.description: Create solid, gradient, or pattern images and save as PNG/JPG/SVG/GIF

imageCompressor.description: Reduce image file size in your browser and download optimized JPG/PNG/WebP files

imageConverter.description: Convert images between JPG, PNG, and WebP directly in your browser

imageResizer.description: Resize images by width, height, or percentage without uploading files

colorConverter.description: Convert colors between HEX, RGB, HSL, and OKLCH directly in your browser

encoderDecoder.description: Encode and decode text with URL encoding, Base64, and HTML entities

jsonFormatter.description: Format, minify, and validate JSON locally in your browser

timestampConverter.description: Convert Unix timestamps to readable dates and back, with local time and UTC

characterCounter.description: Count characters in your text, with and without spaces.

dummyText.description: Generate dummy placeholder text in a language of your choice, at an exact character count

home.heroTitle: Useful tools, only when you need them.

meta.title: oddkit - A small toolkit for work
meta.description: Use small browser-based tools for image generation, compression, conversion, resizing, and everyday work tasks.

dummyImage.title: Dummy Image Generator
dummyImage.description: Create solid, gradient, or pattern images and save in your preferred format.

seo.overview: Create placeholder images for mockups, layouts, tests, and design drafts directly in your browser. Choose a solid color, gradient, or pattern, adjust the size and text, then download the result in your preferred format.
seo.privacy: Images are generated locally in the browser. The selected colors, dimensions, text, and generated files are not uploaded to a server.
seo.formats: PNG, JPG, JPEG, WebP, SVG, and GIF downloads are supported.
seo.embed: Every size and format is also available as a direct image URL — no download or interactive tool needed. Drop it straight into an img tag, README, or mockup.
seo.embedHint: Size: 1–4000px per side. Format: png, jpg, or webp. Cached and served instantly on repeat requests.
useCases: Placeholder images for website and app layouts
Mockup assets for design drafts
Test images for development workflows

faq.question: Can I use the generated images in commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The generated dummy images are created from your selected settings and can be used in your own projects.

faq.question: Are my images uploaded to oddkit?
faq.answer: No. Image generation and downloads run in your browser.

faq.question: Which image formats can I download?
faq.answer: You can download PNG, JPG, JPEG, WebP, SVG, and GIF files.

faq.question: Can I use a placeholder image without opening the tool?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the /placeholder/{width}x{height}.{format} URL directly in an tag — it renders the image on request, no page visit required.

meta.title: Dummy Image Generator - oddkit
meta.description: Create placeholder images with solid colors, gradients, patterns, and text. Download PNG, JPG, WebP, SVG, or GIF files in your browser.

imageCompressor.title: Image Compressor
imageCompressor.description: Reduce image file size locally in your browser. Adjust quality, max dimensions, and output format before downloading the optimized image.

controls.supported: Supports JPG, PNG, and WebP images.
controls.targetHint: oddkit will try to create the best image under {size}.
controls.pngTargetWarning: PNG is lossless, so target size mode may not reduce it reliably. WebP or JPG is recommended.

preview.empty: Upload an image to preview it here.

errors.unsupported: This file type is not supported. Please choose a JPG, PNG, or WebP image.
errors.compress: Unable to compress this image. Try another file or format.

seo.overview: Compress images for websites, apps, emails, and content workflows without uploading files to a server. Choose image quality, set maximum dimensions, and convert the output to JPG, PNG, or WebP.
seo.privacy: Images are processed locally in your browser. The selected file and compressed result are not uploaded to oddkit.
seo.formats: JPG, PNG, and WebP input images are supported. Output can keep the original format or be converted to JPG, PNG, or WebP.
useCases: Reduce website image weight before publishing
Optimize screenshots and content images
Create smaller assets for prototypes and documentation

faq.question: Are my images uploaded to oddkit?
faq.answer: No. Compression runs locally in your browser using Canvas.

faq.question: Which formats can I compress?
faq.answer: You can compress JPG, PNG, and WebP images, then download JPG, PNG, or WebP output.

faq.question: Why can PNG output be larger than the original?
faq.answer: PNG is lossless, so reducing quality may not shrink it like JPG or WebP. Use WebP or JPG for stronger compression.

meta.title: Image Compressor - oddkit
meta.description: Compress JPG, PNG, and WebP images in your browser. Reduce file size, set a target size, and download optimized images without uploading files.

imageConverter.title: Image Converter
imageConverter.description: Convert JPG, PNG, and WebP images locally in your browser. Choose the output format and quality, then download the converted file.

controls.supported: Supports JPG, PNG, and WebP images.

preview.empty: Upload an image to preview it here.

errors.unsupported: This file type is not supported. Please choose a JPG, PNG, or WebP image.
errors.convert: Unable to convert this image. Try another file or format.

seo.overview: Convert images for websites, apps, documents, and content workflows without uploading files to a server. Choose JPG, PNG, or WebP output and tune quality when the format supports it.
seo.privacy: Images are converted locally in your browser. The selected file and converted result are not uploaded to oddkit.
seo.formats: JPG, PNG, and WebP input images are supported. Output can be JPG, PNG, or WebP.
useCases: Create WebP images for faster web pages
Convert screenshots and design assets to JPG or PNG
Prepare compatible image files for forms and documents

faq.question: Are my images uploaded to oddkit?
faq.answer: No. Conversion runs locally in your browser using Canvas.

faq.question: Which formats can I convert?
faq.answer: You can open JPG, PNG, and WebP images, then download JPG, PNG, or WebP output.

faq.question: Why does PNG not have a quality slider?
faq.answer: PNG output is lossless in the browser Canvas API, so quality settings mainly apply to JPG and WebP.

meta.title: Image Converter - oddkit
meta.description: Convert JPG, PNG, and WebP images directly in your browser. Choose output format and quality, then download the converted file.

imageResizer.title: Image Resizer
imageResizer.description: Resize JPG, PNG, and WebP images locally in your browser. Set custom dimensions, keep aspect ratio, and choose the output format.

controls.supported: Supports JPG, PNG, and WebP images.

preview.empty: Upload an image to preview it here.

errors.unsupported: This file type is not supported. Please choose a JPG, PNG, or WebP image.
errors.read: Unable to read this image. Try another file.
errors.resize: Unable to resize this image. Try another file or smaller dimensions.

seo.overview: Resize images for web pages, profiles, documents, thumbnails, and publishing workflows without uploading files to a server. Enter exact dimensions or use percentage presets.
seo.privacy: Images are resized locally in your browser. The selected file and resized result are not uploaded to oddkit.
seo.formats: JPG, PNG, and WebP input images are supported. Output can keep the original format or be converted to JPG, PNG, or WebP.
useCases: Create thumbnails and preview images
Resize profile, banner, and document images
Reduce dimensions before publishing images online

faq.question: Are my images uploaded to oddkit?
faq.answer: No. Resizing runs locally in your browser using Canvas.

faq.question: Can I keep the original aspect ratio?
faq.answer: Yes. Keep aspect ratio is enabled by default and updates the paired dimension automatically.

faq.question: Can resizing also reduce file size?
faq.answer: Often yes. Smaller dimensions usually reduce file size, especially with JPG or WebP output.

meta.title: Image Resizer - oddkit
meta.description: Resize JPG, PNG, and WebP images in your browser. Set width, height, percentage, aspect ratio, and output format without uploading files.

colorConverter.title: Color Converter
colorConverter.description: Convert colors between HEX, RGB, HSL, and OKLCH, preview the result, and copy any value. Everything runs locally in your browser.

controls.supported: Accepts HEX, RGB(A), HSL(A), and OKLCH color values.

errors.invalid: Enter a valid HEX, RGB, HSL, or OKLCH color.

seo.overview: Convert colors between HEX, RGB, HSL, and OKLCH while designing UI, writing CSS, or checking brand colors. Type or pick any supported format and see every equivalent value update instantly.
seo.privacy: Colors are converted locally in your browser. No color values are uploaded to oddkit.
seo.formats: Reads HEX (3/4/6/8 digit), rgb()/rgba(), hsl()/hsla(), and oklch() input, including alpha values, and outputs all four formats.
useCases: Translate design tool colors into CSS-ready values
Check alpha transparency across formats
Explore OKLCH values for modern, perceptually uniform color

faq.question: Are my colors uploaded to oddkit?
faq.answer: No. Conversion runs locally in your browser.

faq.question: Does it support alpha transparency?
faq.answer: Yes. HEX, RGB, HSL, and OKLCH all support an alpha channel, and it carries through every converted value.

faq.question: What happens if I type an invalid color?
faq.answer: The tool shows a clear error message instead of crashing, so you can fix the input without losing your place.

meta.title: Color Converter - oddkit
meta.description: Convert colors between HEX, RGB, HSL, and OKLCH in your browser. Supports alpha values, live preview, and one-click copy.

encoderDecoder.title: Encoder · Decoder
encoderDecoder.description: Encode and decode text using URL encoding, Base64, or HTML entities, all locally in your browser.

errors.invalidUrl: This text isn't valid URL-encoded input. Check for incomplete or malformed % sequences.
errors.invalidBase64: This text isn't valid Base64. Only letters, numbers, +, /, and = are allowed, in groups of 4 characters.
errors.generic: Unable to process this text. Try a different input.

seo.overview: Encode and decode small text snippets for URLs, query strings, embeds, and API payloads without uploading anything to a server. Switch between URL encoding, Base64, and HTML entities, and see the result update instantly.
seo.privacy: Text is encoded and decoded locally in your browser. Nothing you type is uploaded to oddkit.
seo.modes: URL encoding, Base64, and HTML entity encoding are supported, in both directions.
useCases: Prepare query string values for URLs and API requests
Decode Base64 payloads from tokens, logs, or configs
Escape or unescape HTML entities before embedding text in markup

faq.question: Is my text uploaded to oddkit?
faq.answer: No. Encoding and decoding run locally in your browser.

faq.question: What happens if I paste invalid input?
faq.answer: The tool shows a clear error message explaining why the text can't be decoded, instead of silently failing.

faq.question: Can I copy the result?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the copy button to copy the output to your clipboard.

meta.title: Encoder · Decoder - oddkit
meta.description: Encode and decode text with URL encoding, Base64, and HTML entities directly in your browser. Copy the result with one click.

jsonFormatter.title: JSON Formatter & Validator
jsonFormatter.description: Format, minify, and validate JSON locally in your browser. Prettify indentation, compact JSON to one line, or catch syntax errors with line and column details.

seo.overview: Clean up messy API responses, config files, and logs by formatting JSON with consistent indentation, or compact it into a single line for storage and transport. Parsing runs locally using your browser's native JSON engine.
seo.privacy: JSON you paste is processed locally in your browser. It is never uploaded to oddkit's servers.
useCases: Pretty-print API responses for debugging
Minify JSON config before shipping it
Find the exact line causing a JSON syntax error

faq.question: Is my JSON uploaded anywhere?
faq.answer: No. Parsing, formatting, and minifying all happen locally in your browser using JSON.parse and JSON.stringify.

faq.question: What happens if my JSON is invalid?
faq.answer: oddkit shows a validation error with the approximate line and column where parsing failed, without crashing.

faq.question: Can I copy the result?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the Copy output button to copy the formatted or minified JSON to your clipboard.

meta.title: JSON Formatter & Validator - oddkit
meta.description: Format, minify, and validate JSON in your browser. Prettify indentation, compact output, and find syntax errors with line and column details. Nothing is uploaded to a server.

timestampConverter.title: Timestamp Converter
timestampConverter.description: Convert Unix timestamps to readable dates and back, entirely in your browser. Supports seconds and milliseconds, with local time and UTC shown side by side.

errors.not_a_number: Enter a valid number, in seconds or milliseconds.
errors.out_of_range: This value is outside the range JavaScript dates can represent.
errors.dateEmpty: Pick a date and time to convert.
errors.dateInvalid: This date and time could not be parsed.

seo.overview: Convert Unix timestamps to human-readable dates and convert dates back into timestamps, for debugging logs, API payloads, scheduled jobs, and analytics data.
seo.privacy: Timestamps and dates are converted locally in your browser. Nothing you enter is uploaded to oddkit.
useCases: Check what time a Unix timestamp in a log line or API response represents
Convert a scheduled date and time into a timestamp for a cron job or API call
Compare a timestamp across your local time zone, UTC, and another time zone

faq.question: Are my timestamps or dates uploaded to oddkit?
faq.answer: No. All parsing and formatting happens locally in your browser.

faq.question: How does the tool know if a timestamp is in seconds or milliseconds?
faq.answer: By default it auto-detects based on magnitude: present-day second-based timestamps are around 10 digits, while millisecond-based timestamps are around 13 digits. You can also force seconds or milliseconds manually.

faq.question: Can I see a timestamp in a time zone other than my own?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the compare time zone selector to see the same instant formatted in any supported IANA time zone alongside your local time and UTC.

meta.title: Timestamp Converter - oddkit
meta.description: Convert Unix timestamps to readable dates and back in your browser. Supports seconds and milliseconds, local time, UTC, and time zone comparison.

characterCounter.title: Character Counter
characterCounter.description: Count characters in your text locally in your browser, with spaces included and excluded.

seo.overview: Count characters in essays, social posts, meta descriptions, and form fields as you type. Counting runs locally and updates instantly, with a separate count that excludes all whitespace.
seo.privacy: Text you type or paste is processed locally in your browser. It is never uploaded to oddkit's servers.
useCases: Check text length against a character limit before submitting
Compare character counts with and without spaces
Count multi-byte characters like emoji correctly

faq.question: Is my text uploaded anywhere?
faq.answer: No. Counting happens entirely locally in your browser as you type.

faq.question: How is "without spaces" calculated?
faq.answer: All whitespace characters — spaces, tabs, and line breaks — are removed before counting.

faq.question: Does it count emoji correctly?
faq.answer: Yes. Counting uses Unicode code points, so multi-byte characters like emoji count as a single character.

meta.title: Character Counter - oddkit
meta.description: Count characters in your text in your browser, with spaces included and excluded. Nothing is uploaded to a server.

dummyText.title: Dummy Text Generator
dummyText.description: Generate placeholder text in your browser. Choose a language and character count, then copy the result instantly.

seo.overview: Generate placeholder dummy text for mockups, wireframes, and layout testing. Choose a language and the exact character count you need to preview how text renders across different scripts.
seo.privacy: Text is generated locally in your browser. Nothing is uploaded to oddkit's servers.
useCases: Fill design mockups with realistic-looking placeholder copy
Preview how a layout handles different languages and character counts
Generate quick filler content for prototypes and demos

faq.question: Is the generated text sent to a server?
faq.answer: No. All text is generated locally in your browser using JavaScript.

faq.question: Can I control how much text is generated?
faq.answer: Yes. Choose a language and set the exact character count you need.

faq.question: Can I generate placeholder text in different languages?
faq.answer: Yes. Pick from several languages to preview how your layout handles different scripts and character widths.

meta.title: Dummy Text Generator - oddkit
meta.description: Generate dummy placeholder text in multiple languages with an exact character count, directly in your browser. Nothing is uploaded to a server.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: oddkit provides browser-based tools for image generation and editing. Most of what you do in these tools stays on your device — nothing is uploaded to our servers. This policy explains what data we do collect and how it may be used.

sections.heading: Data We Collect
body: oddkit's tools (dummy image generator, image compressor, image converter, image resizer) run entirely in your browser using client-side JavaScript and the Canvas API. Images you upload, generate, or edit are never transmitted to oddkit's servers.
oddkit does not require an account and does not collect names, email addresses, or other personal information to use the tools.
Our hosting provider may temporarily log standard technical data, such as IP address, browser type, and request timestamps, for security and reliability purposes.

sections.heading: Local Storage and Cookies
body: oddkit stores your theme preference (light or dark mode) in your browser's local storage. This data stays on your device and is never sent to us.
If oddkit displays advertising, our advertising partners, including Google, may use cookies to serve ads based on your prior visits to this or other websites.

sections.heading: Advertising and Third-Party Vendors
body: Third-party vendors, including Google, use cookies to serve ads based on a user's prior visits to this website or other websites. Google's use of advertising cookies enables it and its partners to serve ads to you based on your visit to oddkit and/or other sites on the internet.

You may opt out of personalized advertising by visiting Google Ads Settings (https://adssettings.google.com). You can also opt out of third-party vendors' use of cookies for personalized advertising by visiting www.aboutads.info/choices.

sections.heading: Children's Privacy
body: oddkit's tools are not directed at children under 13, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from children.

sections.heading: Changes to This Policy
body: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Changes will be posted on this page with a new revision date.

sections.heading: Contact
body: If you have questions about this privacy policy, contact us at {email}.

meta.title: Privacy Policy - oddkit
meta.description: How oddkit collects, stores, and uses data, including local processing, cookies, and third-party advertising.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: By using oddkit, you agree to the following terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the site.

sections.heading: Description of Service
body: oddkit provides free, browser-based tools for image generation, compression, conversion, and resizing. Processing happens locally in your browser; files are not uploaded to our servers unless a tool explicitly states otherwise.

sections.heading: Acceptable Use
body: You agree not to use oddkit to create, upload, or process unlawful, infringing, or harmful content, and not to attempt to disrupt, reverse engineer, or abuse the service.

sections.heading: Your Content
body: You retain full ownership of any images you create or process using oddkit's tools, including for commercial use. oddkit claims no rights to files you generate or upload.

sections.heading: No Warranty
body: oddkit is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind, express or implied, including fitness for a particular purpose or non-infringement. We do not guarantee the service will be uninterrupted, error-free, or available at all times.

sections.heading: Limitation of Liability
body: To the fullest extent permitted by law, oddkit and its operator are not liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the service.

sections.heading: Advertising
body: oddkit may display advertising, including through Google AdSense, to support the cost of running the service. Advertising is subject to the practices described in our Privacy Policy.

sections.heading: Changes to These Terms
body: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use of oddkit after changes are posted means you accept the revised terms.

sections.heading: Contact
body: Questions about these terms can be sent to {email}.

meta.title: Terms of Service - oddkit
meta.description: The terms and conditions for using oddkit's browser-based image tools.
